requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must maximize 403(b) employee salary deferral contributions for public school employees, non-profit organization employees, and certain ministers to reduce taxable income and build tax-deferred retirement savings
criteria: Must be employed by public school, tax-exempt 501(c)(3) organization, or certain church organizations
2025 employee deferral limit: $23,500 for participants under age 50 (same as 401(k))
Special 15-year catch-up: Additional $3,000 per year for employees with 15+ years service (subject to limits)
Contributions can be traditional (pre-tax) or Roth (after-tax) within aggregate limits
403(b) plans limited to annuity contracts or mutual fund custodial accounts (no individual stocks)
Can contribute to both 403(b) and 457(b) if employer offers both, essentially doubling deferral limits
Consider coordination with employer contributions and other retirement savings opportunities

403b_contribution_rules.15_year_catchup: Additional $3,000 annually for employees with 15+ years service (lifetime limit $15,000)
403b_contribution_rules.dual_plan_opportunity: Can maximize both 403(b) and 457(b) if employer offers governmental 457(b) plan
403b_contribution_rules.tax_deduction: Traditional contributions reduce taxable income providing immediate tax savings
403b_contribution_rules.roth_option: Roth 403(b) contributions provide tax-free growth and distributions
403b_contribution_rules.employer_contributions: Employer contributions (if any) don't count toward employee deferral limit

restrictions: 15-year catch-up limited to $3,000 per year and $15,000 lifetime; cannot use simultaneously with age 50+ catch-up
Total contributions (employee + employer) limited to lesser of $70,000 (2025) or 100% compensation
Investment options limited to annuities or mutual funds; cannot invest in individual securities
Higher fees common in 403(b) plans, especially older annuity contracts; review expenses carefully
Early withdrawal before age 59½ subject to 10% penalty except for specific exceptions
Required minimum distributions begin at age 73 (age 75 if born 1960+)
Job change: can roll over to IRA, new employer 403(b), or 401(k); avoid taxable distribution
Some 403(b) plans have limited investment options or high-cost annuity products
